annotations.label: C*-algebra (informal)

anchor.snippet: is a Banach algebra together with an involution satisfying the properties of the adjoint

annotations.note: `CStarAlgebra` is a complex Banach algebra with isometric involution satisfying the C⋆-identity, exactly the informal notion (with `NonUnitalCStarAlgebra` for the non-unital case).

annotations.label: Continuous functions vanishing at infinity

anchor.snippet: Another important class of non-Hilbert C*-algebras includes the algebra

annotations.note: An instance makes `C₀(α, A)` a `NonUnitalCommCStarAlgebra`, giving the vanishing-at-infinity functions as a (non-unital, commutative) C⋆-algebra.

annotations.label: C*-algebra (abstract)

anchor.snippet: is a Banach algebra over the field of complex numbers

annotations.note: `CStarAlgebra` is precisely a complex Banach ⋆-algebra with the C⋆-identity, matching the abstract characterization.

annotations.label: C* identity

anchor.snippet: The last identity is called the C* identity

annotations.note: `CStarRing.norm_star_mul_self : ‖x⋆ * x‖ = ‖x‖ * ‖x‖` is exactly the C⋆-identity (the `CStarRing` class encodes the defining inequality).

annotations.label: C*-norm uniquely determined

anchor.snippet: it implies that the C*-norm is uniquely determined by the algebraic structure

annotations.note: The fact that injective ⋆-homomorphisms are isometric (`NonUnitalStarAlgHom.norm_map`) implies uniqueness of the C⋆-norm, but the uniqueness statement itself is not separately stated.

annotations.label: *-homomorphism

anchor.snippet: is called a *-homomorphism

annotations.note: `StarAlgHom` (and `NonUnitalStarAlgHom`) are algebra homomorphisms intertwining the involution, i.e. ⋆-homomorphisms.

annotations.label: *-homomorphisms are contractive/isometric

anchor.snippet: any *-homomorphism π between C*-algebras is contractive

annotations.note: `NonUnitalStarAlgHom.norm_apply_le` shows ⋆-homs are contractive and `NonUnitalStarAlgHom.norm_map` shows injective ones are isometric.

annotations.label: C*-isomorphism

anchor.snippet: is called a C*-isomorphism

annotations.note: `StarAlgEquiv` is a bijective ⋆-algebra homomorphism, i.e. a C⋆-isomorphism.

annotations.label: B*-algebra

anchor.snippet: to describe Banach *-algebras that satisfy the condition

annotations.note: Mathlib only formalizes the C⋆-axiom (`CStarRing`); the equivalent B*-condition `‖x⋆ x‖ = ‖x⋆‖‖x‖` is not defined as a class (it is an explicit TODO in the file).

annotations.label: B*-condition equivalent to C*-condition

anchor.snippet: This condition automatically implies that the *-involution is isometric

annotations.note: `CStarRing.norm_star_mul_self'` and `norm_star` give the B*-form and isometry of `star` within a `CStarRing`, but the equivalence of the B*- and C*-conditions is explicitly listed as a TODO.

annotations.label: C*-algebra (Segal)

anchor.snippet: to describe norm-closed subalgebras of B

annotations.note: `StarSubalgebra.cstarAlgebra` shows a norm-closed ⋆-subalgebra of a C⋆-algebra is a C⋆-algebra, which applied to `B(H)` gives Segal's concrete definition.

annotations.label: Self-adjoint elements

anchor.snippet: Self-adjoint elements are those of the form

annotations.note: `IsSelfAdjoint x ↔ star x = x` (and the subgroup `selfAdjoint`) formalizes self-adjoint elements.

annotations.label: Positive elements form a closed convex cone

anchor.snippet: forms a closed convex cone

annotations.note: `CStarAlgebra.isClosed_nonneg` proves the nonnegative set is closed and the `StarOrderedRing` structure gives the cone/convexity, but no single `ConvexCone` object packages this.

annotations.label: Non-negative elements

anchor.snippet: Elements of this cone are called non-negative

annotations.note: Nonnegativity `0 ≤ a` is defined via the spectral `StarOrderedRing` order and characterized by `nonneg_iff_isSelfAdjoint_and_quasispectrumRestricts`.

annotations.label: Partial ordering of self-adjoint elements

anchor.snippet: naturally has the structure of a partially ordered vector space

annotations.note: `CStarAlgebra.spectralOrder`/`spectralOrderedRing` give the partial order on the whole algebra, but the ordered-vector-space structure on `selfAdjoint A` is not packaged separately.

annotations.label: Characterization of positivity

anchor.snippet: if and only if the spectrum of

annotations.note: This lemma states `0 ≤ a` iff `a` is self-adjoint with nonnegative spectrum, exactly the spectral characterization of positivity.

annotations.label: Existence of approximate identity

anchor.snippet: Any C*-algebra A has an approximate identity

annotations.note: `CStarAlgebra.increasingApproximateUnit` proves every C⋆-algebra has an increasing approximate unit (`CStarAlgebra.approximateUnit`), formulated via filters.

annotations.label: Sequential approximate identity

anchor.snippet: In case A is separable, A has a sequential approximate identity

annotations.note: Mathlib's approximate unit is filter-based and there is no result deriving a countable/sequential approximate identity for separable C⋆-algebras.

annotations.label: Quotient by closed ideal is a C*-algebra

anchor.snippet: the algebraic quotient of a C*-algebra by a closed proper two-sided ideal

annotations.note: No C⋆-algebra (or even Banach-algebra) instance is provided for quotients of C⋆-algebras by closed two-sided ideals.

annotations.label: Closed ideal is a C*-algebra

anchor.snippet: a closed two-sided ideal of a C*-algebra is itself a C*-algebra

annotations.note: Closed non-unital ⋆-subalgebras are shown to be `NonUnitalCStarAlgebra`, but it is not proven that a closed two-sided ideal is automatically ⋆-closed and hence a C⋆-algebra.

annotations.label: Matrix algebra M(n,C)

anchor.snippet: becomes a C*-algebra if we consider matrices as operators

annotations.note: `Matrix.instCStarRing` makes `Matrix n n 𝕜` a `CStarRing` under the L²-operator norm, realizing matrices as operators.

annotations.label: Finite-dimensional C*-algebras are direct sums of matrix algebras

anchor.snippet: all C*-algebras that are finite dimensional as vector spaces are of this form

annotations.note: The classification of finite-dimensional C⋆-algebras as direct sums of matrix algebras is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Artin–Wedderburn type theorem

anchor.snippet: A finite-dimensional C*-algebra, A , is canonically isomorphic to a finite direct sum

annotations.note: The structure theorem giving the canonical decomposition of a finite-dimensional C⋆-algebra into matrix blocks is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dimension vector

anchor.snippet: is called the dimension vector of A

annotations.note: The dimension vector of a finite-dimensional C⋆-algebra is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: †-algebra

anchor.snippet: is the name occasionally used in physics

annotations.note: The physics term †-algebra (finite-dimensional C⋆-algebra) has no dedicated formalization.

annotations.label: Bounded operators B(H)

anchor.snippet: The prototypical example of a C*-algebra is the algebra B(H)

annotations.note: An instance gives `CStarAlgebra (E →L[ℂ] E)` for a Hilbert space `E`, i.e. `B(H)` is a C⋆-algebra.

annotations.label: Gelfand–Naimark theorem

anchor.snippet: every C*-algebra, A , is *-isomorphic to a norm-closed adjoint closed subalgebra

annotations.note: The GNS construction (`gnsStarAlgHom`) produces ⋆-representations on Hilbert space, but the universal faithful representation embedding every C⋆-algebra into `B(H)` is not assembled.

annotations.label: Compact operators K(H)

anchor.snippet: The algebra K ( H ) of compact operators on H is a norm closed subalgebra

annotations.note: Compact operators exist only as the predicate `IsCompactOperator`; `K(H)` as a norm-closed C⋆-subalgebra of `B(H)` is not constructed.

annotations.label: Characterization of C*-subalgebras of K(H)

anchor.snippet: If A is a C*-subalgebra of K

annotations.note: The structural classification of C⋆-subalgebras of `K(H)` is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Approximate identity for K(H)

anchor.snippet: a sequential approximate identity for K

annotations.note: The sequential approximate identity for `K(H)` (increasing finite-rank projections) is not formalized.

annotations.label: K(H) is the unique ideal of B(H)

anchor.snippet: is a two-sided closed ideal of B

annotations.note: The fact that `K(H)` is the unique nontrivial closed two-sided ideal of `B(H)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Calkin algebra

anchor.snippet: is the Calkin algebra

annotations.note: The Calkin algebra `B(H)/K(H)` is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Commutative C*-algebra C0(X)

anchor.snippet: forms a commutative C*-algebra

annotations.note: `C₀(α, ℂ)` is a `NonUnitalCommCStarAlgebra` (and `C(α, ℂ)` a `CommCStarAlgebra` for compact `α`), the commutative C⋆-algebra of functions.

annotations.label: Unit exists iff X compact

anchor.snippet: has a multiplicative unit element if and only if

annotations.note: There is no theorem stating `C₀(X)` is unital iff `X` is compact.

annotations.label: Gelfand representation

anchor.snippet: every commutative C*-algebra is *-isomorphic to the algebra

annotations.note: `gelfandStarTransform` is a `StarAlgEquiv` between a unital commutative C⋆-algebra and `C(characterSpace ℂ A, ℂ)`, formalizing the Gelfand representation in the unital case.

annotations.label: Universal enveloping C*-algebra

anchor.snippet: there is a unique (up to C*-isomorphism) C*-algebra E ( A ) and *-morphism

annotations.note: The universal enveloping C⋆-algebra of a Banach ⋆-algebra is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: C*-enveloping algebra

anchor.snippet: is called the C*-enveloping algebra

annotations.note: The C⋆-enveloping algebra is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: C*-algebra of a locally compact group

anchor.snippet: This is defined as the enveloping C*-algebra of the group algebra

annotations.note: The group C⋆-algebra is not formalized (no enveloping C⋆-algebra of the group algebra exists).

annotations.label: Von Neumann algebra

anchor.snippet: are a special kind of C*-algebra

annotations.note: `VonNeumannAlgebra H` (double-commutant ⋆-subalgebra of `B(H)`) and the abstract `WStarAlgebra` formalize von Neumann/W*-algebras.

annotations.label: Sherman–Takeda theorem

anchor.snippet: any C*-algebra has a universal enveloping W*-algebra

annotations.note: The Sherman–Takeda theorem (universal enveloping W*-algebra as the bidual) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Type I C*-algebra

anchor.snippet: A C*-algebra A is of type I if and only if for all non-degenerate representations

annotations.note: Type I C⋆-algebras are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Type I locally compact group

anchor.snippet: A locally compact group is said to be of type I if and only if its group C*-algebra is type I

annotations.note: Type I locally compact groups are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Glimm's dichotomy

anchor.snippet: it also has representations of type II and type III

annotations.note: Glimm's dichotomy for non-type-I C⋆-algebras is not formalized.

annotations.label: Observables

anchor.snippet: are thought of as the observables

annotations.note: The physical notion of observables (self-adjoint elements as measurements) has no dedicated formalization, though `IsSelfAdjoint` exists.

annotations.label: State of the system

anchor.snippet: A state of the system is defined as a positive functional on A

annotations.note: Positive linear functionals `A →ₚ[ℂ] ℂ` are formalized (`PositiveLinearMap`), but a normalized `State` (positive functional of norm one) is not defined as such.
